acceptance.after: Global offset 0: ours_frame = PB_rally_local + 45. u/v correlation r=0.9492; court residual median 5.67 px; uncertainty ±1 frame.

Report coverage, pixel errors, and asymmetric misses.

acceptance.after: 172 non-interpolated pairs. Only overlapping normal-fit segment: median 3D delta 4.65 m, height bias -0.347 m. Fallback: median/p95 delta 5.57/23.89 m, height bias +7.923 m. Run1/run6 core numeric artifacts identical.
acceptance.target: Compare normal-fit and fallback output with Bland-Altman and robustness measurements.

acceptance.after: PB interpolated=true: 1/173 emitted=0.58%; 79/252 rally frames omit 3D. Our fallback: 263/300=87.67%, or 229/252=90.87% during PB rally.
acceptance.target: Quantify interpolation, omission, and fallback coverage.

acceptance.after: Two common events. PB landing errors 0.377/0.064 m, mean 0.220 m; ours 0.306/0.231 m, mean 0.269 m.
acceptance.target: Score both systems against shared calibrated-plane pseudo-GT.

acceptance.after: Center difference 0.0269 m; horizontal-FOV difference 0.274°; model-to-model court projection median/p95/max=8.93/13.00/13.63 px.
PB rally 8.366 s and 57.32 ft=17.471 m movement; ours covers about 10.0 s and totals 19.626 m. IDs/windows are not directly matched.
acceptance.target: Brief comparable stats table without false ranking.

honest_issues: PB is a comparator, not ground truth.
Only normal-fit segment 5 overlaps PB 3D; segment 1 cannot be compared.
The PB Euler convention was inferred from court correspondences because the export does not document it.
Bounce pseudo-GT uses PB event pixels and our calibration, so it is not independent.
Run6 lacks run1's physics-filled, net-plane, and virtual-world artifacts; robustness applies to the available BALL/CAL/arc/render chain.
Our calibration remains metric_confidence=low; VERIFIED=0 remains binding.
